expectations.evidence: Line 3 of output.md: "Hi Bob," — a clear professional greeting addressing the recipient by name.
Lines 9-10 of output.md: "Best regards,\n[Your Name]" — a standard professional sign-off with a name placeholder.

expectations.text: The output is properly formatted with paragraphs
expectations.evidence: output.md uses blank lines to separate the subject line, greeting, body paragraph, call-to-action paragraph, and sign-off into distinct visual blocks. Each section is separated by a blank line, forming proper paragraph structure.

claims.claim: The format identified is Professional Email per SKILL.md rules
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 3 identifies the format as Professional Email. The output follows standard email structure with subject, greeting, body, and sign-off.

claims.claim: Every fact from the input is represented in the output
claims.evidence: Input mentions Bob (present as recipient), Q4 numbers (present in subject and body), and they look off (reworded as 'discrepancies that warrant a closer look'). All facts preserved.

claims.claim: Tone is consistently professional with no slang
claims.evidence: The output uses formal phrasing ('reach out regarding', 'discrepancies that warrant', 'schedule some time'). No slang or casual language present.
Subject is 'Q4 Numbers Review' and the body discusses reviewing Q4 numbers — they align.

suggestions.reason: This assertion is trivially satisfied — any output containing the word 'Hello', 'Hi', or 'Dear' passes regardless of whether the greeting is correctly addressed to the right person. Consider checking that the greeting addresses 'Bob' specifically, matching the input.
Similarly weak — any closing phrase passes. A stronger check would verify the sign-off follows the SKILL.md rules (sign-off phrase AND sender name on the next line).

suggestions.assertion: The output is properly formatted with paragraphs
suggestions.reason: Vague and easy to satisfy. Any output with two newlines somewhere passes. Consider checking for specific structural elements: subject line on the first line, blank-line-separated sections, and that the email has at minimum a subject, greeting, body, and sign-off block.

No assertion checks that the output preserves the factual content from the input (Q4 numbers, the concern that they look off, and the recipient Bob). An output that produces a perfectly formatted but content-irrelevant email would pass all three assertions.
No assertion checks that the tone was transformed from casual to professional. The skill's purpose is tone transformation, yet the evals only check structural formatting.

eval_feedback.overall: All three assertions test surface-level structure and are trivially satisfiable. They would pass for almost any email-shaped output regardless of whether the skill correctly transformed the input. The evals need content-verification and tone-checking assertions to be discriminating.
